title: SSE GET and POST share one HTTP security boundary

preconditions.description: The harness holds the inherited device-keyed lease before any device or fixture access.
The harness recorded session generation, display, layout, lock state, ATX-observable state, browser identity, and fixture identity.
The legacy SSE adapter has deterministic authentication, Host, Origin, anti-CSRF policy, allocation, and lookup counters.

fault_script.action: Omit authentication from GET /sse while Host, Origin, and anti-CSRF policy are otherwise valid and reset both counters to zero.
fault_script.expected_effect: The adapter returns 401 Unauthorized with Connection: close before stream allocation or routing lookup.

fault_script.action: Omit authentication from POST /messages while presenting a syntactically valid unknown routing identifier and otherwise valid policy.
fault_script.expected_effect: The adapter returns 401 Unauthorized with Connection: close before routing lookup; both counters remain zero.

fault_script.action: Authenticate GET /sse with an opaque fixture principal but reject its Host policy before stream creation.
fault_script.expected_effect: The adapter returns 403 Forbidden with Connection: close before stream allocation or routing lookup; both counters remain zero.

fault_script.action: Authenticate POST /messages with an opaque fixture principal but omit its required anti-CSRF proof while keeping Host and Origin allowed.
fault_script.expected_effect: The adapter returns 403 Forbidden with Connection: close before routing lookup; both counters remain zero.

fault_script.action: Restore valid authentication, Host, Origin, and anti-CSRF policy for the next GET and POST and issue the deterministic synthetic routing identifier 11111111-1111-4111-8111-111111111111.
fault_script.expected_effect: GET allocates exactly one stream, then POST performs exactly one lookup through the same boundary and reaches that stream.

steps.expect: HTTP 401 with body Unauthorized and Connection: close; stream_allocation_count 0 and routing_lookup_count 0.
HTTP 401 with body Unauthorized and Connection: close; stream_allocation_count 0 and routing_lookup_count 0.
HTTP 403 with body Forbidden and Connection: close; stream_allocation_count 0 and routing_lookup_count 0.
HTTP 403 with body Forbidden and Connection: close; stream_allocation_count 0 and routing_lookup_count 0.
HTTP 200 text/event-stream with endpoint /messages?sessionId=11111111-1111-4111-8111-111111111111; stream_allocation_count 1 and routing_lookup_count 0.
HTTP 202 with body Accepted; stream_allocation_count 1 and routing_lookup_count 1 after the same security boundary.

pass.assertion: get-unauthorized-before-allocation returns exactly 401 Unauthorized with Connection: close and both counters zero.
post-unauthorized-before-lookup returns exactly 401 Unauthorized with Connection: close and both counters zero.
get-forbidden-before-allocation returns exactly 403 Forbidden with Connection: close and both counters zero.
post-forbidden-before-lookup returns exactly 403 Forbidden with Connection: close and both counters zero.
secure-sse-get is the first allocation and secure-messages-post is the first lookup; both traverse the same authentication, Host, Origin, and anti-CSRF boundary.

evidence.source: get-unauthorized-before-allocation: 401 Unauthorized, Connection: close, allocation 0, lookup 0.
post-unauthorized-before-lookup: 401 Unauthorized, Connection: close, allocation 0, lookup 0.
get-forbidden-before-allocation: 403 Forbidden, Connection: close, allocation 0, lookup 0.
post-forbidden-before-lookup: 403 Forbidden, Connection: close, allocation 0, lookup 0.
secure-sse-get and secure-messages-post: exactly one allocation followed by exactly one lookup after four denied calls.

restore.action: Invoke generation-correlated emergency input release when a session exists.
restore.assertion: No held key, button, wheel producer, macro, or paste producer remains.

restore.action: Stop and join any Reliable Paste producer.
restore.assertion: Reliable Paste is inactive and its correlated producer is joined.

restore.action: Verify the fixture reports zero held keys and buttons.
restore.assertion: Held keyboard and pointer state is empty.

restore.action: Close only the device session created by this story and its browser resources.
restore.assertion: No story-owned browser, channel, or device session remains.

restore.action: Clear deterministic faults and restore the recorded fixture baseline.
restore.assertion: The next story starts only after the safe baseline is verified.

privacy.rule: Persist only allowlisted opaque identifiers, sanitized versions, counts, durations, hashes, outcomes, verification, and restoration results.
